content: Comparative studies of intrinsic curiosity mechanisms (ICM vs RND) can be reframed as a testbed for measuring how well a general agent's active memory architecture preserves uncertainty signals for long-horizon episodic planning. Instead of only benchmarking exploration reward, we can rank curiosity methods by how they shape an agent's long-term memory of unresolveable unknowns to drive more efficient open-ended learning.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
